title: Moderate prenatal alcohol exposure differentially alters acute ethanol sensitivity of GABAergic transmission in CRFR1- and CRFR1+ CeM Neurons.
summary: This study investigates how moderate prenatal alcohol exposure (PAE) alters acute ethanol modulation of GABAergic neurotransmission in the central amygdala (CeM) of adult rats, specifically looking at CRFR1+ and CRFR1- neurons.
key_findings: PAE dysregulates acute EtOH's modulation of GABA transmission within the CeM in a sex-specific and cell-type-dependent manner.
Long-term PAE effects in the CeM are sex-specific.
